title: Ultrasonographic measurements of the right femoral vein cross-sectional area
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7j00p","text":"Exclusion criteria: lower limb varices, history of leg surgery, trauma, infection, or thromboembolic events.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5i42h","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5v9f7","text":"Ultrasound examinations are performed in B-mode technique, equipped with a linear broadband (7.5-14-MHz) transducer. The right femoral vein and the inguinal ligament are examined in a supine position. The inguinal ligament is identified along its longitudinal axis. The measurements levels are identified as follow:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"55kt2","text":"-    proximal level: 20 mm caudally to the inguinal ligament identified along the femoral artery imaged with US","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"57cbm","text":"-    distal level: 20 mm caudally to the inguinal crease visually identified and along the femoral artery imaged with US","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7m8nm","text":"The cross-sectional area (CSA) of the right femoral vein is examined at both levels in transverse plane and is measured automatically after manual tracing of the vessel on a static image in three leg positions: abduction, abduction + external rotation, abduction + external rotation + 90º knee flexion/frog-leg. Attention must be taken to avoid any compression or displacement of the examined vein.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
creator: Dorota Czyzewska
